methods.name: addLinear
methods.description: Add a new linear axis configuration for a kinematics part.

methods.name: addRotary
methods.description: Add a new rotary axis configuration for a kinematics part.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Get the configuration at index in this collection

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Get the configuration with the given ID

properties.name: count
properties.description: Get the number of configurations in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addLinear.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.addLinear Method
addLinear.description: Add a new linear axis configuration for a kinematics part.
addLinear.return_description: Returns the newly created LinearMachineAxisConfiguration or null if creation failed.

parameters.name: partId
parameters.description: ID used to label this axis configuration and link to a part in the kinematics tree. partID must match a part of type AxisMachinePartType in the kinematics tree and the part must be a linear axis.

addRotary.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.addRotary Method
addRotary.description: Add a new rotary axis configuration for a kinematics part.
addRotary.return_description: Returns the newly created RotaryMachineAxisConfiguration or null if creation failed.

parameters.name: partId
parameters.description: ID used to label this axis configuration and link to a part in the kinematics tree. partID must match a part of type AxisMachinePartType in the kinematics tree and the part must be a rotary axis.

classType.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.item Method
item.description: Get the configuration at index in this collection

itemById.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.itemById Method
itemById.description: Get the configuration with the given ID
itemById.return_description: Return the MachineAxisConfiguration with the given ID, or null if the given ID does not match any configuration in the collection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID for the configuration to get.

count.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.count Property
count.description: Get the number of configurations in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineAxisConfigurations_var.count

objectType.title: MachineAxisConfigurations.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
